name: Browser Automation Agent
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o Vision for screenshot analysis, action planning, and DOM reasoning

prod.note: ~2M tokens/day, GPT-4o vision + GPT-4o-mini for planning

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for low-latency browser interactions

services.name: Azure Container Apps
services.purpose: Headless browser runtime with Playwright, agent orchestrator, and API gateway

prod.note: 2 vCPU, 4GB per instance, min 2 replicas for browser pool

enterprise.note: 4 vCPU, 8GB, browser pool with 5+ instances, multi-region

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Screenshot storage, page snapshots, session recordings for audit

prod.note: ~500GB, lifecycle policy moves old screenshots to Cool

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Browser session state, task queue, action history, and agent memory

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Browser authentication credentials, target site API keys, session tokens

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Browser action traces, vision API latency, agent decision logging

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Browser container logs, Playwright traces, failure diagnostics

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for simple action planning, reserve GPT-4o Vision only for screenshot analysis — saves 60%
Compress screenshots to 512px before sending to Vision API — reduces image token cost by 75%
Pool headless browser instances across requests — avoids cold-start latency and container churn
Move old screenshots to Cool storage tier after 7 days — saves 50% on storage
Cache DOM structures for frequently visited pages — reduces Vision API calls by 30-40%
Use Container Apps scale rules based on browser session queue depth, not CPU
